methods.description: This method describes the process of collecting, preparing, and analyzing pollen samples from Cactaceae flowers to study their morphological characteristics.

steps.description: Flowers were collected in the Andean zone of Tarapacá Region (~19°S), the coastal area of Coquimbo Region and from the Herbario of Laboratorio of Palaeoclimate and Palaeoecology of CEAZA.
steps.searchable_summary: collect flower samples from specified regions

steps.description: Two flowers of each species were processed in the lab following established standard procedures including KOH (10%) and acetolysis.
steps.searchable_summary: process flower samples using KOH and acetolysis

steps.description: Pollen samples were then mounted in glycerin.
steps.searchable_summary: mount processed pollen samples in glycerin

steps.description: Morphological characteristics of the pollen including number of units, polarity, shape, number and type of apertures as well as the description of the pollen's exine and sculpture were studied for a minimum of 10 grains each under optical microscopy under CARL ZEISS Axiostar plus microscope with 1000x optical zoom following the terminology of Punt et al. (2007).
steps.searchable_summary: analyze pollen morphology using optical microscopy

steps.description: Further analysis and description was based on the inspection of photographs taken with Canon PowerShot A620 under the same microscope and 1000x optical zoom and also following the terminology of Punt et al. (2007).
steps.searchable_summary: analyze pollen photographs for further morphological details

methods.description: This method involves plotting and analyzing the geographical distribution of Cactaceae species using various data sources and GIS tools.

steps.description: Maps of the geographical distribution of the Cactaceae were plotted based on the information taken from various sources (Pinto & Kirberg 2009, Hoffmann & Walter 2004, Riedemann et al. 2006, Señoret Espinosa & Acosta Ramos 2013).
steps.searchable_summary: gather geographical distribution data from literature

steps.description: Graphically implemented in distribution maps using ArcGIS.

Distribution patterns were determined mainly by latitudinal and altitudinal limits to the plants' ecological niche applied on a digital elevation model of Northern Chile, less areas of absolute desert of the Atacama.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze distribution patterns using ecological niche and elevation model

steps.description: The produced maps were then revised for verification using own and external census data (Guerrero et al. 2011).
steps.searchable_summary: verify distribution maps using census data
